title: Aviation Investigation Final Report - WPR24LA299 Colusa, California
defining_event: Loss of control in flight (collective bounce)
known_caveats: Tables reformatted to markdown for readability
Original PDF hyperlinks converted to markdown format
Some spacing and formatting approximated from PDF extraction
Acronyms used without full expansion per original document (AAIP, VMC, KOVE, etc.)
Technical references to CFR sections and FAA regulations preserved verbatim
accessibility_notes: Fully extracted and converted to markdown. All structured tables, lists, and factual data accessible. No OCR artifacts.
pressure_points: Dense structured data (tables with pilot/aircraft specs) mixed with narrative prose
Causal reasoning chain (pilot error → collective bounce → loss of control → crash)
Source credibility and limitations (NTSB did not travel to scene, operator's report vs NTSB findings)
Multiple timeline references and date inference (departure time, medical exam date, last inspection, accident time)
Regulatory knowledge (Part 137 operations, FAA certification, airworthiness requirements)
Contradiction: operator claim ('no preaccident malfunctions') vs NTSB finding (pilot failure to set friction)
investigation_class: Class 4 (the NTSB did not travel to the scene)
